justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated with options and recommendation. Clock-start ambiguity identified (assumptions section notes '2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup' and edge cases explore pickup timing impact). Wrong-address exception from Finance noted as BR-6. Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict identified as a formal escalation with tiered resolution options. 8 business rules extracted with correct invariant classifications. 4 formal escalations provided. Confidence level is 35, well below the 50 threshold.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is the centerpiece escalation, with three resolution options and a practical hybrid recommendation
Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing contradiction explicitly escalated as a separate item with its own options
Wrong-address refund exception captured as BR-6 with correct invariant=false classification
8 business rules extracted, exceeding the minimum of 5, with thoughtful invariant justifications
Confidence at 35 appropriately reflects the severity of unresolved contradictions
Excellent edge case coverage including boundary conditions (exactly 10km, exactly 2 hours), cascading failures (multiple driver cancellations), and gaps (stock-out, address disputes)
Clock-start ambiguity addressed in assumptions ('2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup') and edge case exploring 14-minute pickup impact on remaining delivery time
Rejection reasons clearly explain why the spec cannot proceed to implementation without resolving the contradictions
Pickup SLA breach failure mode identified as a missing escalation — no stakeholder defined what happens when 15 minutes is exceeded

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated (escalation #1), clock-start ambiguity identified and escalated (escalation #4), wrong-address exception from Finance noted (BR-6), auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted (escalation #2), 9 rules extracted with correct classifications, 6 formal escalations, confidence.level=35 which is <=50.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is the lead escalation with three resolution options and a clear recommendation — exactly the kind of domain authority expected from Bird.
Clock-start ambiguity gets its own dedicated escalation (#4) with options ranging from order placement to driver pickup.
Wrong-address exclusion captured as BR-6 with correct invariant=false classification and a testable assertion.
Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing conflict explicitly called out as escalation #2, correctly identifying they describe fundamentally different refund mechanisms.
9 business rules extracted (exceeds the 5 minimum), all with invariant classifications and testable assertions.
6 formal escalations covering contradiction, missing context, and ambiguity — well above the 2 minimum.
Confidence at 35 appropriately reflects the severity of unresolved contradictions.
Excellent edge cases including late-window driver cancellation, midnight boundary, simultaneous refund triggers, and stock-out scenarios.
Rejection reasons clearly explain why acceptance criteria cannot be written, which is the correct call given the contradictions.

BR-4 (driver rating >= 4 stars) classified as invariant=false with sound reasoning that the threshold could be adjusted based on driver supply — a defensible judgment call showing domain reasoning rather than mechanical extraction.
Distance measurement ambiguity (geodesic vs driving) is a sophisticated catch not in the rubric but highly relevant to operational feasibility.

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated (escalation #1), clock-start ambiguity identified (assumptions section and edge cases), wrong-address exception from Finance noted (BR-6), auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted (escalation #2), 8 rules extracted with correct invariant classifications, 5 formal escalations provided, confidence.level=35 which is <=50.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee contradiction is the lead escalation with three resolution options and a recommendation — textbook Bird escalation behavior.

Clock-start ambiguity addressed in assumptions ('2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time') and implicitly in the delivery window definition, though not surfaced as a standalone escalation.
Wrong-address exclusion captured as BR-6 and given its own escalation (#3) with adjudication process options.
Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing conflict explicitly escalated (#2) with clear description of the PM/CS vs Finance tension.

8 business rules extracted, all with correct invariant classifications — BR-1 (10km, invariant:true), BR-2 (fee, invariant:false), BR-3 (rating, invariant:true), BR-4 (pickup SLA, invariant:false — debatable but defensible), BR-5/6/7/8 (refund policies, invariant:false).
5 formal escalations covering contradiction, two ambiguities, and two missing contexts — well above the 2 minimum.
Confidence at 35 with strong reasoning about why it's low, plus well-separated high/low confidence areas.
9 edge cases identified, several of which go beyond the rubric requirements (driver rating drop mid-delivery, sequential cancellations, warehouse closing time).
Rejection reasons section explicitly blocks acceptance criteria until the core contradiction is resolved — strong domain authority behavior.
Clock-start ambiguity could have been a standalone escalation rather than buried in assumptions. The rubric expected it surfaced more prominently, though it is identified.

BR-4 pickup SLA classified as invariant:false is debatable — Operations described it as an express SLA requirement, suggesting it's a hard operational constraint. The justification ('missing it degrades service but does not corrupt system state') is reasonable but could be challenged.
